subject: LU-12861 libcfs: provide an scnprintf and start using it

values.-1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1

I was just thinking of doing this. From linux/Documentation/filesystems/sysfs.txt

- show() must not use snprintf() when formatting the value to be
  returned to user space. If you can guarantee that an overflow
  will never happen you can use sprintf() otherwise you must use
  scnprintf().

Patch Set 3:

I should point something does need to be added to spelling.txt

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

This change cannot be cherry-picked to master.
Please rebase the change locally and upload the rebased commit for review.

Patch Set 3:

> I should point something does need to be added to spelling.txt

Sorry I'm not sure I understand. Currently there is an sprintf||snprintf would you suggest changing it to sprintf||scnprintf ?

parents.subject: LU-12634 llite: Use __xa_set_mark if it is available

commit.subject: LU-12861 libcfs: provide an scnprintf and start using it
commit.message:
LU-12861 libcfs: provide an scnprintf and start using it

snprintf() returns the number of chars that would be needed to hold
the complete result, which may be larger that the buffer size.

scnprintf differs in it's return value is number of chars actually
written (not including the terminating null).

Correct the few patterns where the return from snprintf() is used and
expected not to exceed the passed buffer size.

Test-Parameters: trivial
Cray-bug-id: LUS-7999
